items.title: Woman's life 'changed for the better' after GPs in SA trained to diagnose ADHD

children.content: About 100 South Australian GPs are now qualified to diagnose ADHD and provide treatment, with the hope of that number doubling by the end of the year, with one patient describing her diagnosis through her GP as "life-changing".

gallery.altText: A person standing in the Adelaide CBD.

thumbnail.altText: A person standing in the Adelaide CBD.

items.title: Childcare company CEO denies problems at centres a symptom of growing pains

children.content: The head of Edge Early Learning says he is conducting a "whole of business transformation project" after being the subject of compliance action over inadequate supervision and other issues at its childcare centres in Adelaide.

image.altText: A sign saying EDGE in front of a childcare centre

items.title: Uncounted SA votes in the 'order of dozens', Attorney-General says

children.content: The number of newly revealed uncounted ballot papers from the March state election is believed to be in the "order of dozens", South Australia's Attorney-General says.

image.altText: A person wearing a blue vest with the words Electoral Officer on it among people lining up at a polling booth

items.title: Gift bags and cruise ship fallout part of Adelaide's COP31 considerations

children.content: A heavily redacted feasibility study into Adelaide's capacity to host the COP31 UN climate summit provides a fleeting glimpse into what was under consideration — including politically fraught floating hotels.

image.altText: Adelaide River Torrens rowing

items.title: Tim Picton's accused killer pleads guilty over separate assault

children.content: A man who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton in Perth admits assaulting another man in Kalamunda just days earlier.

image.altText: Brodie Dewar leaves court surrounded by reporters holding microphones.

items.title: Meet the eco-venture turning an invasive pest into dog treats

children.content: Introducing the European carp to Australian waterways is an ecological disaster that continues to worsen, but an Adelaide couple have found a novel use for the pest.

gallery.altText: Young woman feeds a kelpie a treat

thumbnail.altText: Young woman feeds a kelpie a treat

items.title: Farmers fear potential mouse plague as CSIRO warns of high numbers

children.content: As if the cost of diesel and the lack of fertiliser were not enough, South Australia's farmers are also facing the prospect of a mouse plague.

image.altText: a close up of a man's hands holding a live mouse

items.title: SA carer allegedly sexually assaulted woman with disability, police say

children.content: A 55-year-old man from Adelaide's west has been arrested and charged over the alleged sexual assault of a woman with a disability at Glenelg.

image.altText: The South Australia Police logo on a building.

items.title: The artworks we can't stop thinking about from the Adelaide Biennial

children.content: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

image.altText: Archie Moore, an Indigenous man, mid-50s, in a cap, holds a heart made of gold in his purple-gloved hands.

items.title: Hospital cancels birthing services again, forcing expectant parents to travel

children.content: Two regional hospitals, 30 minutes' drive apart, struggle to consistently provide routine medical services, forcing repeated patient diversions.

image.altText: A pregnant woman's belly as she holds hands with someone

items.title: As flight options shrink, this regional city is hoping for a return to rail

children.content: Trains stopped coming to Mount Gambier decades ago, but with fewer flights, locals want them back.

image.altText: A man wearing a red and brown striped jumper stands underneath a black sign with white writing which reads "Mount Gambier"

items.title: 'It's just bulldozed through': Native title holders allege cultural sites have been destroyed by mining company

children.content: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

image.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

items.title: Iran's meme factory is using 'the weapons of their enemy' to embarrass Trump

children.content: AI-generated Lego memes criticising the conflict in the Middle East and mocking Donald Trump are attracting a large audience online as a new front opens in the Iran war.

image.altText: AI-generated image of Donald Trump animated in a lego-style with a terrified expression.

gallery.altText: A close up of a Lego figure that looks like Trump sweating and holding up a sign.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a Lego figure that looks like Trump sweating and holding up a sign.

avatar.alt: A close up photo of a brunette woman wearing glasses.

items.title: Gaza conditions 'catastrophic' as report finds $US71b needed to rebuild

children.content: More than $US71 billion will be needed over the next 10 years for recovery and reconstruction in Gaza, according to a new report by the European Union and the United Nations.

image.altText: Palestinian woman in a headscarf looks out a from a window of a tent set up inside her war-damaged home.

items.title: The growing problem stopping Melbourne residents using their microwaves

children.content: A power company warns that growing electricity demand is creating an undervoltage issue as Victoria's infrastructure struggles to keep up with the transition from gas.

image.altText: Powerlines about a suburban street

items.title: How young boys helped Australians wage guerilla war against Japan in Timor

children.content: Incredible bonds were formed between Australian commandos and Timorese boys during World War II. The White Australia policy meant they had to say goodbye when the Anzacs departed.

image.altText: A graphic shows a contemporary colour of a landscape in Timor with a black and white photo of a boy and soldier in the middle.

items.title: Recordings point to second Queensland population of rare night parrot

children.content: Ecologists say thousands of hours of audio recordings have detected night parrot calls in south-west Queensland.

image.altText: small bird on the ground beside bush.

items.title: The Michael Jackson biopic is as generic as its subject was singular

children.content: Spanning from the Jackson 5's 1960s discovery through to the height of his fame in the late 80s, Michael skates over the more challenging aspects of the King of Pop.

gallery.altText: A man in a red jacket smiles at the camera while four other men stand behind him.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a red jacket smiles at the camera while four other men stand behind him.

items.title: Staff face misconduct proceedings after foster kids allowed to live with killer

children.content: Some Department of Communities and Justice staff members will face misconduct proceedings after a bungle that saw two foster children living with triple killer Regina Arthurell earlier this year.

image.altText: A selfie picture of a person in blonde hard and big dark sunglasses

items.title: Aussie junior 'obsessed' with golf set to test his skills overseas

children.content: Alby Weyden was two years old when he became obsessed with golf. Four years on, his family hopes to raise enough money for him to compete in the Canadian Invitational.

image.altText: A young boy sitting next to his golf clubs while on the course

items.title: 'How many beers have you had?' Flood evacuees claim racial profiling

children.content: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

articles.title: New $30.5m funding package to help repair NT roads damaged by flooding

children.content: The Northern Territory government has announced $30.5 million in funding to repair roads damaged by wet season flooding across the territory.

image.altText: A wet, damaged road has chunks of bitumen missing.

gallery.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

thumbnail.altText: Signs attached to black fencing reads: no alcohol, no visitors

items.title: 'It's been horrible': Your experiences with family estrangement

children.content: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

image.altText: An illustration of a woman sitting on a park bench looking update with her hands on her head

items.title: $50/hour charge for older Australians to shower reversed by government after six months

children.content: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

image.altText: A close shot of a husband and wife holding each others hands.

items.title: In a split second, Keelie made the 'surreal' decision to reveal her abuse

children.content: As Australia marks Sexual Assault Awareness Month, and experts and victim-survivors say sexual violence remains pervasive and under-reported.

gallery.altText: a woman with clasped hands on her lap

thumbnail.altText: a woman with clasped hands on her lap

items.title: 15yo among five charged over alleged kidnapping of rapper's bodyguard

children.content: Four men and a teenage boy are set to front court today over the alleged assault and kidnapping of a bodyguard for rapper and alleged Alameddine associate Ali Younes.

image.altText: Forensic detectives remove evidence from an abduction scene.

items.title: 'I cannot afford to lose': Moloney's slow path back to boxing summit

children.content: Jason Moloney knows the path back to the summit of world boxing is paved with pain, but he will take another step back in Brisbane on Thursday night.

image.altText: Jason Moloney stands on the ring corner

items.title: Three weeks' pay guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers

children.content: A short-term rescue package announced by the Tasmanian and federal governments will guarantee the wages of workers at the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter.

image.altText: A large manufacturing building.

items.title: US authorities probe possible ChatGPT involvement in university shooting

children.content: US authorities are investigating if there was any criminal behaviour involving interactions between Open AI's ChatGPT app and a man accused of killing two people and injuring six others in a shooting at Florida State University.

image.altText: James Uthmeier standing in front of a microphone wearing a suit.

items.title: Remote residents 'cooking from the inside' sue NT government

children.content: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say scorching outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they're suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.
A box air conditioner on the outside of a house, balanced on an ironing board and a table.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.
A box air conditioner on the outside of a house, balanced on an ironing board and a table.
